title: fix: rank free models first by pollen value
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Marks catalog models with explicit zero pricing as free.
- Shows free models as `∞ gen/pollen` and sorts them above finite values in descending order.
- Keeps `—` for models that genuinely lack usage data.
- Adds regression coverage for catalog detection, display, and ranking.

Root cause: the usage statistics query excludes zero-price requests, so free models had no average-cost row and were treated as missing.

Checks:
- `npx biome check --write ...`
- `npx vitest run enter.pollinations.ai/test/model-calculations.test.ts`
- `npm run typecheck --workspace pollinations-enter`

gist.headline: Free models find the sunshine
gist.blurb: The model catalog now recognizes zero-priced models as properly free, rather than mistaking their lack of billing data for a mysterious blank spot. Infinite generations per Pollen: mathematically rude, visually clear.
gist.summary: Fixed the model catalog so explicitly zero-priced models display as “∞ gen/pollen” and rank above finite generation-per-Pollen values, while models with genuinely missing usage data still show “—”.
gist.impact: Users can now spot free models at the top of the catalog instead of seeing them treated as if their usage information were unavailable.
gist.visual_concept: Depict the model catalog as a cozy garden leaderboard: Polli, wearing tiny librarian glasses, places a glowing lime-green “∞ gen/pollen” seed packet at the very top of a wooden ranking board. Below it are orderly finite-value seed packets, while a separate small “—” sign rests beside an unlabeled pot to represent genuinely missing data; code-plants and flowers make the sorting feel organic.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polli, the cute yellow-and-brown striped bee mascot with pink cheek blush and light blue wings, wearing tiny librarian glasses as she places a glowing lime green (#ecf874) seed packet marked “∞ gen/pollen” at the top of a wooden garden ranking board; finite seed packets sit neatly below, and one empty flower pot has a small “—” label. Code-plants, pastel mint and lavender flowers, and a warm retro terminal create a friendly catalog garden. Cozy pixel art, 8-bit aesthetic, soft pastel gradients, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes, nostalgic but beautiful.
